message:
perf(bench): add dirty-row vs full diff comparison benchmarks

Adds comprehensive benchmarks comparing BufferDiff::compute() against
BufferDiff::compute_dirty() to quantify dirty-row optimization benefits.

New benchmark groups:

diff/full_vs_dirty:
- Compare both methods on 200x60 and 240x80 screens
- Test 5% sparse changes (typical interactive use)
- Test single-row changes (cursor movement, status updates)
- Measures throughput in cells/second for direct comparison

diff/large_screen:
- Regression detection for 4K-like terminal sizes
- Tests 320x90 and 400x100 buffers
- 2% change rate (conservative typical use case)
- Both compute() and compute_dirty() variants

Expected results:
- Sparse changes: compute_dirty() 2-5x faster
- Single-row changes: compute_dirty() 10-50x faster
- Full changes: Both methods equivalent (dirty falls back to full)
- Large screens: Dirty optimization increasingly beneficial

Run benchmarks with:
  cargo bench -p ftui-render --bench diff_bench

Use criterion's comparison feature to track regressions:
  cargo bench -p ftui-render --bench diff_bench -- --save-baseline main

These benchmarks support the bd-3e1t.1.6 bead requirement for validating
the dirty-row diff optimization strategy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
